title: NOC 11201 – Professional Occupations in Business Management Consulting | GTR Immigration
description: Professionals classified under NOC 11201 are key drivers of organizational efficiency, regulatory compliance, and operational transformation in Canada. With strong post-pandemic growth across consulting firms, corporations, and government agencies, this occupation offers excellent opportunities for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) applicants.

classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree
classification.minor_group: 1120 – Human resources and business service professionals

duties: Professionals in this group may perform some or all of the following:
Analyze and advise on managerial methods and organizational structures
Research the effectiveness of management policies and programs
Recommend improvements to systems in operations, HR, security, and communications
Conduct ISO quality audits and design assurance frameworks
Support regulatory compliance and prepare filings with authorities
Plan corporate reorganizations and restructuring strategies
Supervise project teams or research staff
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree or college diploma in business administration, commerce, or related fields
Graduate degree in business administration (MBA) for senior roles
Certification by provincial management consulting associations (in some cases)
CHIMA certification for health-information consulting roles
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create 68,700 new jobs for professionals in business management consulting, while approximately 63,800 workers are expected to enter the field. For internationally trained candidates—particularly from India—this represents strong long-term immigration potential with the right planning and credential alignment.
labour_market_demand: Business management consultants remain in strong demand nationwide as organizations focus on digital transformation, process optimization, compliance, and restructuring.
High hiring levels across consulting firms and corporations
Excellent immigration prospects for internationally trained professionals
Annual earnings typically range from $44,500 to $120,300, depending on region, experience, and specialization.
Secure professional roles in both public and private sectors
Strong labour protections and work-life balance
Direct pathways to Canadian Permanent Residence (PR)
